Reduced Inequalities.reason: Highly relevant by covering 'measures of national wealth' and 'monetary and fiscal policy,' which are primary tools for understanding and creating policies to reduce income inequality (Target 10.4).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course covers foundational concepts of 'scarcity' and 'Production and Growth,' which relate to resource efficiency (Target 12.2), but lacks an explicit focus on sustainability.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Moderately relevant by analyzing 'the economic role of the government' and 'The Monetary System,' which are crucial for building effective and accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Highly relevant, with a strong focus on 'international trade and finance' and 'Open-economy macroeconomics,' which are central to the global trading system and macroeconomic stability (Targets 17.10, 17.13).

Clean Water and Sanitation.reasoning: There is no explicit or inferred content in the course syllabus related to water, sanitation, hygiene, or water resource management.
